title: 3 Legal Considerations for BPRP
body_text:
3	Legal Considerations for BPRP
3.1	Summary of Legal Considerations for BPRP
Financial institutions must comply with all applicable legislation and regulations. The information provided in this Chapter is for convenience only and does not take the place of statutes and regulations. Refer to the actual statute or regulation for the full text. There is no guarantee of accuracy for the online statutes.
Note:	Other federal and provincial legislation influences and regulates bill payment transactions (e.g., privacy legislation, human rights legislation). Your financial institution must apply additional policies and procedures to address these aspects.
3.2	CPA Rules
Financial institutions offering BPRP must comply with CPA Rule H6, Rules Pertaining to the Inter Financial Institution Exchange of Bill Payment Remittances for the Purpose of Clearing and Settlement. This rule applies to paper and electronic payments and includes, but is not limited to, the following requirements:
A Corporate Creditor Agreement is entered into with each corporate creditor as follows:
â
a separate agreement per remittance type (for example, property tax and utilities), or
â
a single agreement for several types of remittances provided that each remittance has a separate Corporate Creditor Identification Number (CCIN).
A CCIN is obtained for the corporate creditor during enrollment, if one doesnât already exist.
Refer to Payments Canadaâs website for more information on CPA Rule H6, âRules Pertaining to the Inter Financial Institution Exchange of Bill Payment Remittances for the Purpose of Clearing and Settlementâ.
3.3	Restricted Entities
Financial institutions must exercise enhanced due diligence to identify Restricted Entities when processing applications from new billers.
While due diligence is ultimately your financial institutionâs responsibility, Central 1 reserves the right to further investigate Restricted Entities and may decline to provide services to entities deemed to be outside of Central 1âs risk appetite. Central 1 may make inquiries on specific transactions and ask for the results of your financial institutionâs enhanced due diligence efforts.
END
